prompt: Tell me a bio of Chadwick Boseman
expected_answer: Factual accuracy of birth/death dates, nationality, profession (actor, director, writer), notable film roles (Black Panther, 42, Get On Up), career timeline, awards, and achievements. Avoid subjective claims about cultural impact unless directly attributed to named sources. Dates of major films and career milestones must be precise.
actual_output:
## Chadwick Boseman — Biography

**Chadwick Aaron Boseman** (November 29, 1976 – August 28, 2020) was an American actor and playwright whose two-decade career spanned blockbuster and independent films. He is best known for portraying T'Challa / Black Panther in the Marvel Cinematic Universe and for his powerful portrayals of iconic Black historical figures. [数据来源: Wikipedia — Chadwick Boseman]

---

### Early Life and Education

Boseman was born on November 29, 1976, in **Anderson, South Carolina**, where he was raised. His mother, **Carolyn**, was a nurse, and his father, **Leroy**, worked at a textile factory and managed an upholstery business. As a child, he practiced martial arts and initially aspired to become an architect. [数据来源: Wikipedia]

He graduated from **T. L. Hanna High School** in 1995, where he played on the basketball team and competed in Speech and Debate, placing eighth in Original Oratory at the 1995 National Tournament. In his junior year, after a classmate was shot and killed, he wrote his first play, *Crossroads*, and staged it at the school. [数据来源: Wikipedia]

Though recruited to play basketball in college, Boseman chose the arts. He attended **Howard University** in Washington, D.C., graduating in 2000 with a **Bachelor of Fine Arts in Directing**. His teachers included **Al Freeman Jr.** and **Phylicia Rashad**, who became a mentor. With help from Rashad — and funding raised by **Denzel Washington** — Boseman attended the **British American Drama Academy's Oxford Summer Program** at Balliol College, England, in 1998. He also traveled to **Ghana** during college to work on preserving and celebrating rituals through performance, an experience he called "one of the most significant learning experiences of [his] life." He later completed additional film studies at New York City's **Digital Film Academy**. [数据来源: Wikipedia]

---

### Theater and Early Career (2000–2007)

Boseman began his career in **Brooklyn, New York**, focusing on theater. In 2000, he was named a **Drama League Directing Fellow**. He directed productions including George C. Wolfe's *The Colored Museum* and Amiri Baraka's *Dutchman*. He worked as a drama instructor at the **Schomburg Junior Scholars Program** in Harlem (2002–2009). [数据来源: Wikipedia]

He rose to prominence as a playwright and stage actor in 2002, winning an **AUDELCO** award for his performance in Ron Milner's *Urban Transitions*. As a member of the National Shakespeare Company of New York, he played Romeo in *Romeo and Juliet* and Malcolm in *Macbeth*. His play *Deep Azure*, blending hip-hop culture and Egyptian deities, earned a **Jeff Award** nomination in 2005. [数据来源: Wikipedia]

---

### Film Breakthrough: Portraying Historical Figures (2008–2015)

Boseman transitioned to screen acting with appearances in television, including the NBC drama *Persons Unknown* (2010). His breakthrough came when he was cast as baseball legend **Jackie Robinson** in the film **42** (2013). He continued to portray iconic Black historical figures:

- **James Brown** in *Get on Up* (2014)
- **Thurgood Marshall** in *Marshall* (2017)

These performances established Boseman as a powerful leading man capable of embodying real-life legends. [数据来源: Wikipedia]

---

### Black Panther and Global Stardom (2016–2019)

Boseman achieved **international fame** for playing **T'Challa / Black Panther** in the Marvel Cinematic Universe. His first appearance was in *Captain America: Civil War* (2016), followed by the landmark standalone film **Black Panther** (2018), directed by Ryan Coogler. As the **first Black actor to headline an MCU film**, Boseman became a global cultural icon. *Black Panther* was a critical and commercial phenomenon, breaking box office records. [数据来源: Wikipedia, Web Search]

He reprised the role in *Avengers: Infinity War* (2018) and *Avengers: Endgame* (2019). In 2018, he was named to the **Time 100** list of most influential people. [数据来源: Wikipedia]

---

### Illness and Final Performances (2016–2020)

In 2016, Boseman was diagnosed with **colon (colorectal) cancer**. He kept his condition entirely private, continuing to work and make public appearances throughout his four-year battle. Even as he underwent surgeries and chemotherapy, he delivered some of his most celebrated performances. [数据来源: Wikipedia]

His final film role was as **Levee Green** in **Ma Rainey's Black Bottom** (2020), an adaptation of August Wilson's play, co-starring Viola Davis and directed by George C. Wolfe. The performance was widely hailed as his finest. For this role, he received:
- **Golden Globe Award for Best Actor – Drama** (posthumous)
- **Screen Actors Guild Award for Best Actor** (posthumous)
- A **posthumous Academy Award nomination** for Best Actor

He also posthumously won a **Primetime Emmy Award** for Outstanding Character Voice-Over Performance for his work as T'Challa in the Disney+ series *What If...?* (2021). [数据来源: Wikipedia]

---

### Death and Legacy

Chadwick Boseman died on **August 28, 2020**, at his home in **Los Angeles, California**, at the age of **43**. His death shocked the world, as almost no one outside his inner circle knew of his illness. He is buried in Anderson, South Carolina. [数据来源: Wikipedia]

He married singer **Taylor Simone Ledward**, who was by his side during his illness. [数据来源: Wikipedia]

In November 2025, Boseman was posthumously honored with a **star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame**. Marvel Studios chose not to recast the role of T'Challa out of respect for Boseman's memory. His portrayal of Black Panther became a symbol of Black pride and representation in cinema, inspiring millions worldwide. [数据来源: Web Search — QQ News, IMDb]

---

### Key Filmography

| Year | Film/Show | Role |
|------|-----------|------|
| 2013 | *42* | Jackie Robinson |
| 2014 | *Get on Up* | James Brown |
| 2016 | *Captain America: Civil War* | T'Challa / Black Panther |
| 2017 | *Marshall* | Thurgood Marshall |
| 2018 | *Black Panther* | T'Challa / Black Panther |
| 2018 | *Avengers: Infinity War* | T'Challa / Black Panther |
| 2019 | *Avengers: Endgame* | T'Challa / Black Panther |
| 2019 | *21 Bridges* | Andre Davis |
| 2020 | *Da 5 Bloods* | "Stormin'" Norman Earl Holloway |
| 2020 | *Ma Rainey's Black Bottom* | Levee Green |
| 2021 | *What If...?* | T'Challa / Black Panther (voice) |

[数据来源: Wikipedia — Chadwick Boseman]
